prompts.task: Instrument the Go key-value store in ./service to reduce mean time to detect asynchronous persistence failures, search-index backpressure or staleness, and capacity eviction. Add safe app-owned detector-ready OpenTelemetry signals in addition to baseline HTTP/runtime telemetry. Preserve the existing cmd/kvstore-server startup path and package APIs, and do not invent filesystem or platform signals the service cannot observe.

judge_inputs: Inspect ./service for resulting source, configuration, tests, and .observe reports.
Read ./last_message.md for the agent final response.
rubric: Preserves cmd/kvstore-server and the kvstore package boundary while adding baseline OTLP setup, low-cardinality otelhttp server telemetry, resource identity, propagation, shutdown, and the existing launch/runtime integration.

Adds detector-ready app-owned signals for asynchronous persistence failures and outcomes, index queue pressure or active backlog, and capacity eviction; where useful it also exposes bounded persistence duration or active work without spawning telemetry goroutines per observation.

Uses accurate observable state such as channel depth/capacity, active persistence work, and explicit failure/eviction events; it does not claim filesystem capacity, durable-write success, or index freshness that the code cannot actually observe.
Keeps metric attributes bounded to operation, outcome, reason family, or component and never uses key, value, search term, raw path, request ID, goroutine ID, or error text as metric attributes.
Adds focused tests for the custom metric-producing persistence, queue/backpressure, and eviction paths and runs go test with the race detector or records a concrete blocker with command evidence.

Writes proof-aware instrumentation and verification handoff material that names exact signals, distinguishes MTTD-improving detector inputs from localization-only telemetry, and identifies any remaining filesystem/platform prerequisite.
